title: cestrum jacaltenanginum var. jacaltenanginum
intro: Cestrum jacaltenanginum var. jacaltenanginum, also known as the Mexican stinkbush or Jacaltenango stinkbush, is a plant species in the family Solanaceae, native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cestrum jacaltenanginum var. jacaltenanginum include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
